skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI
skill.summary: Create professional AI videos using 40+ models including Veo 3.1, Wan 2.5, and Grok Imagine with text-to-video, image-to-video, and lipsync capabilities
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 62 patterns (44 external_commands, 16 network, 2 cryptographic) but all are false positives. The skill file is documentation-only containing markdown examples for the inference.sh CLI. No executable code, no command injection vectors, and no actual network calls. URLs are documentation references. Safe for publication.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: Markdown code blocks showing CLI usage examples, not executable code
Documentation URLs and example image/audio URLs in command templates, not actual network calls

content.value_statement: Creating professional videos is expensive and time-consuming. This skill enables instant video generation using 40+ AI models including Google Veo, Wan, and Grok through a simple CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using Google Veo 3.1, Veo 3, and Grok Imagine Video
Animate images into videos using Wan 2.5 and Seedance models
Create talking avatars with lipsync using OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse
Add sound effects to silent videos using HunyuanVideo Foley
Upscale video quality with Topaz Video Upscaler
Merge multiple videos with transitions using Media Merger
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI account and API credits to function
Video generation is asynchronous - results are not instant
Cannot generate videos without valid image URLs or text prompts
Model availability and pricing depend on inference.sh platform

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate engaging short-form videos for social platforms using text prompts describing your desired content
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Product Demo Videos
use_cases.description: Create professional product demonstration videos from images and scripts without filming equipment

use_cases.title: Educational Content
use_cases.description: Build explainer videos with AI avatars that speak your narration for courses and training materials

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video with the prompt: "A timelapse of a flower blooming in a garden"
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick video generation from a simple text description

prompt_templates.title: Image Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image into a video: [image URL] using Wan 2.5 with natural motion
prompt_templates.scenario: Bringing static images to life with realistic movement

prompt_templates.title: Talking Avatar
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking avatar video using [portrait image URL] and [audio URL] with OmniHuman 1.5
prompt_templates.scenario: Making a person in an image speak with provided audio

prompt_templates.title: Complete Video Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video from "cyberpunk city street at night", add foley sound effects for traffic and neon signs, then upscale to 4K quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-step workflow combining generation, sound, and enhancement

output_examples.input: Generate a video: "drone shot flying over a forest"
output_examples.output: Video file URL returned from Google Veo 3.1 Fast with 5-second aerial forest footage
MP4 video URL showing the input image brought to life with natural motion

best_practices: Use specific, descriptive prompts with clear visual details for better video quality
Test with shorter durations first before generating longer videos to save credits
Provide high-quality input images for image-to-video and avatar generation
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts like "make a video" without describing the content
Avoid using copyrighted images or audio without proper licensing
Do not expect photorealistic results for highly complex or abstract concepts

faq.question: What is inference.sh and do I need an account?
faq.answer: Inference.sh is an API platform providing access to AI models. You need to create an account and install their CLI tool to use this skill.

faq.question: How much does video generation cost?
faq.answer: Pricing varies by model and video length. Check inference.sh pricing page for current rates. Shorter videos cost less.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Generation time varies from 30 seconds to several minutes depending on model complexity and video length.

faq.question: Can I generate videos without showing my face?
faq.answer: Yes, text-to-video models like Google Veo generate videos from text prompts without requiring any images of you.

faq.question: What video formats are supported?
faq.answer: Generated videos are typically delivered as MP4 files compatible with all major video players and social platforms.

faq.question: Can I use generated videos commercially?
faq.answer: Usage rights depend on the specific model and inference.sh terms. Check the model documentation and your subscription plan for commercial usage rights.
